citations.title: Applicability
citations.text: Applicability

citations.title: Definitions
citations.text: Definitions

citations.title: Security standards: General rules
citations.text: Security standards: General rules

citations.title: General requirements
citations.text: Covered entities and business associates must do the following:

citations.title: Flexibility of approach
citations.text: (1) Covered entities and business associates may use any security measures that allow the covered entity or business associate to reasonably and appropriately implement the standards and implementation specifications as specified in this subpart.

citations.title: Standards
citations.text: A covered entity or business associate must comply with the applicable standards as provided in this section and in §§ 164.308, 164.310, 164.312, 164.314 and 164.316 with respect to all electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Implementation specifications
citations.text: In this subpart:

citations.title: Clause 1
citations.text: Implementation specifications are required or addressable. If an implementation specification is required, the word “Required” appears in parentheses after the title of the implementation specification. If an implementation specification is addressable, the word “Addressable” appears in parentheses after the title of the implementation specification.

citations.title: Clause 2
citations.text: When a standard adopted in § 164.308, § 164.310, § 164.312, § 164.314, or § 164.316 includes required implementation specifications, a covered entity or business associate must implement the implementation specifications.

citations.title: Clause 3
citations.text: When a standard adopted in § 164.308, § 164.310, § 164.312, § 164.314, or § 164.316 includes addressable implementation specifications, a covered entity or business associate must—

citations.title: Maintenance
citations.text: A covered entity or business associate must review and modify the security measures implemented under this subpart as needed to continue provision of reasonable and appropriate protection of electronic protected health information, and update documentation of such security measures in accordance with § 164.316(b)(2)(iii).

citations.title: Administrative safeguards
citations.text: Administrative safeguards

citations.title: Security management process
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.

citations.title: Risk analysis
citations.text: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information held by the covered entity or business associate.

citations.title: Risk management
citations.text: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with § 164.306(a).

citations.title: Sanction policy
citations.text: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.

citations.title: Information system activity review
citations.text: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

citations.title: Assigned security responsibility
citations.text: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by this subpart for the covered entity or business associate.

citations.title: Workforce security
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of its workforce have appropriate access to electronic protected health information, as provided under paragraph (a)(4) of this section, and to prevent those workforce members who do not have access under paragraph (a)(4) of this section from obtaining access to electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Authorization and/or supervision
citations.text: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with electronic protected health information or in locations where it might be accessed.

citations.title: Workforce clearance procedure
citations.text: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to electronic protected health information is appropriate.

citations.title: Termination procedures
citations.text: Implement procedures for terminating access to electronic protected health information when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends or as required by determinations made as specified in paragraph (a)(3)(ii)(B) of this section.

citations.title: Information access management
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

citations.title: Isolating health care clearinghouse functions
citations.text: If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, the clearinghouse must implement policies and procedures that protect the electronic protected health information of the clearinghouse from unauthorized access by the larger organization.

citations.title: Access authorization
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to electronic protected health information, for example, through access to a workstation, transaction, program, process, or other mechanism.

citations.title: Access establishment and modification
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's or the business associate's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

citations.title: Security awareness and training
citations.text: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of its workforce (including management).

citations.title: Implementation specifications
citations.text: Implement:

citations.title: Security reminders
citations.text: Periodic security updates.

citations.title: Protection from malicious software
citations.text: Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.

citations.title: Log-in monitoring
citations.text: Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.

citations.title: Password management
citations.text: Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.

citations.title: Security incident procedures
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

citations.title: Response and reporting
citations.text: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known to the covered entity or business associate; and document security incidents and their outcomes.

citations.title: Contingency plan
citations.text: Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence (for example, fire, vandalism, system failure, and natural disaster) that damages systems that contain electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Data backup plan
citations.text: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Disaster recovery plan
citations.text: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.

citations.title: Emergency mode operation plan
citations.text: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of the security of electronic protected health information while operating in emergency mode.

citations.title: Testing and revision procedures
citations.text: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.

citations.title: Applications and data criticality analysis
citations.text: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.

citations.title: Evaluation
citations.text: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and, subsequently, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of electronic protected health information, that establishes the extent to which a covered entity's or business associate's security policies and procedures meet the requirements of this subpart.

citations.title: Business associate contracts and other arrangements
citations.text: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit electronic protected health information on the covered entity's behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances, in accordance with § 164.314(a), that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information. A covered entity is not required to obtain such satisfactory assurances from a business associate that is a subcontractor.

citations.title: Written contract or other arrangement
citations.text: Document the satisfactory assurances required by paragraph (b)(1) or (b)(2) of this section through a written contract or other arrangement with the business associate that meets the applicable requirements of § 164.314(a).

citations.title: Physical safeguards
citations.text: Physical safeguards

citations.title: Facility access controls
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to its electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

citations.title: Contingency operations
citations.text: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.

citations.title: Facility security plan
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.

citations.title: Access control and validation procedures
citations.text: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control, and control of access to software programs for testing and revision.

citations.title: Maintenance records
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security (for example, hardware, walls, doors, and locks).

citations.title: Workstation use
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Workstation security
citations.text: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access electronic protected health information, to restrict access to authorized users.

citations.title: Device and media controls
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain electronic protected health information into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

citations.title: Disposal
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of electronic protected health information, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.

citations.title: Media re-use
citations.text: Implement procedures for removal of electronic protected health information from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

citations.title: Accountability
citations.text: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore.

citations.title: Data backup and storage
citations.text: Create a retrievable, exact copy of electronic protected health information, when needed, before movement of equipment.

citations.title: Technical safeguards
citations.text: Technical safeguards

citations.title: Access control
citations.text: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in § 164.308(a)(4).

citations.title: Unique user identification
citations.text: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

citations.title: Emergency access procedure
citations.text: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary electronic protected health information during an emergency.

citations.title: Automatic logoff
citations.text: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

citations.title: Encryption and decryption
citations.text: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Audit controls
citations.text: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

citations.title: Integrity
citations.text: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.

citations.title: Mechanism to authenticate electronic protected health information
citations.text: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that electronic protected health information has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

citations.title: Person or entity authentication
citations.text: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

citations.title: Transmission security
citations.text: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

citations.title: Integrity controls
citations.text: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted electronic protected health information is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

citations.title: Encryption
citations.text: Implement a mechanism to encrypt electronic protected health information whenever deemed appropriate.

citations.title: Organizational requirements
citations.text: Organizational requirements

citations.title: Business associate contracts or other arrangements
citations.text: The contract or other arrangement required by § 164.308(b)(3) must meet the requirements of paragraph (a)(2)(i), (a)(2)(ii), or (a)(2)(iii) of this section, as applicable.

citations.title: Business associate contracts
citations.text: The contract must provide that the business associate will—

citations.title: Requirements for group health plans
citations.text: Except when the only electronic protected health information disclosed to a plan sponsor is disclosed pursuant to § 164.504(f)(1)(ii) or (iii), or as authorized under § 164.508, a group health plan must ensure that its plan documents provide that the plan sponsor will reasonably and appropriately safeguard electronic protected health information created, received, maintained, or transmitted to or by the plan sponsor on behalf of the group health plan.
The plan documents of the group health plan must be amended to incorporate provisions to require the plan sponsor to—

citations.title: Other arrangements
citations.text: The covered entity is in compliance with paragraph (a)(1) of this section if it has another arrangement in place that meets the requirements of § 164.504(e)(3).

citations.title: Business associate contracts with subcontractors
citations.text: The requirements of paragraphs (a)(2)(i) and (a)(2)(ii) of this section apply to the contract or other arrangement between a business associate and a subcontractor required by § 164.308(b)(4) in the same manner as such requirements apply to contracts or other arrangements between a covered entity and business associate.

citations.title: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements
citations.text: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Policies and procedures
citations.text: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of this subpart, taking into account those factors specified in § 164.306(b)(2)(i), (ii), (iii), and (iv). This standard is not to be construed to permit or excuse an action that violates any other standard, implementation specification, or other requirements of this subpart. A covered entity or business associate may change its policies and procedures at any time, provided that the changes are documented and are implemented in accordance with this subpart.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Documentation
citations.text: (i) Maintain the policies and procedures implemented to comply with this subpart in written (which may be electronic) form; and
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Time limit
citations.text: Retain the documentation required by paragraph (b)(1) of this section for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Availability
citations.text: Make documentation available to those persons responsible for implementing the procedures to which the documentation pertains.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Updates
citations.text: Review documentation periodically, and update as needed, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of the electronic protected health information.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Policies and procedures and documentation requirements

citations.title: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards
citations.text: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards

citations.title: Health plan
citations.text: (1) A health plan that is not a small health plan must comply with the applicable requirements of this subpart no later than April 20, 2005.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards

citations.title: Health care clearinghouse
citations.text: A health care clearinghouse must comply with the applicable requirements of this subpart no later than April 20, 2005.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards

citations.title: Health care provider
citations.text: A covered health care provider must comply with the applicable requirements of this subpart no later than April 20, 2005.
citations.sourceSectionTitle: Compliance dates for the initial implementation of the security standards
